basics.title: The basics
basics.subtitle: Give this situation a name so you can reuse it across plans.
basics.descriptionPlaceholder: Anything helpful to remember about this situation.

income.title: What you can invest
income.subtitle: How much you can put toward investing each month. We don't ask for your salary.
income.monthlyLabel: How much do you invest per month?
income.monthlyHint: This is the amount you set aside to invest.
income.monthlyError: Enter how much you can invest.
income.varyTitle: Does it vary month to month?
income.varySubtitle: Optional · adjust how much you invest in specific months.
income.varyHint: 1.0 = your typical amount. 0.5 = half that month.
income.growthSubtitle: Optional · how much you increase what you invest over time.
income.variableSubtitle: Optional · one-off or month-to-month contributions (e.g. you invest a bonus).

accounts.title: Where you invest
accounts.subtitle: List each account or portfolio you're saving into.
accounts.correlationSubtitle: Optional · advanced. Leave alone if unsure.

savings.title: Savings & cash
savings.subtitle: Savings accounts, term deposits and money-market funds. Barely moves.

conservative.title: Conservative
conservative.subtitle: Fixed-income / bond mutual funds. Small ups and downs.

balanced.title: Balanced
balanced.subtitle: Mixed funds — stocks and bonds together. A steady mix.

aggressive.title: Aggressive
aggressive.subtitle: Stocks and global equity funds or ETFs. Bigger swings, more growth.

correlation.hint: −1 means they move opposite, 0 means independent, +1 means they move together.
